metadata.title: Exploring Career Paths with Career Cards: A Career Guide for School Students
metadata.publisher: National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT)
metadata.development_institute: PSS Central Institute of Vocational Education (PSSCIVE), Bhopal
metadata.target_audience: Secondary and Higher Secondary School Students (Grades 9-12)
metadata.purpose: Career guidance and counselling for school students. Each entry covers: description, personality traits, scholarships, loans, course fees, educational pathway, expected salary, career growth path, work environment, recommended institutes (govt & private), and real-world examples.

careers.title: Agricultural Engineer
careers.description: An agricultural engineer is a professional who applies and uses the principles of agriculture and engineering to come up with solutions in the sector. They design and develop new farming technologies that help in making farming more efficient in terms of sustainability, safety and environment-friendly. Their roles entail designing, constructing and modernizing farming equipment and machinery to improve land use, increase yields, and conserve resources.
personality_traits: You are interested in science and technology
You like to solve practical problems
You care about the environment and sustainability
You enjoy working both indoors and outdoors
You pay attention to details while working
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in the Science stream.
2. Complete Bachelor's degree in Agricultural
Engineering and Technology/Biotechnology.
Complete Bachelor's degree and follow it up with
Master's in Agriculture Engineering.
Complete Diploma in Agriculture Engineering.
For admissions, you must qualify JEE Main. JEE
Advanced is needed for admissions to IITs. Other
entrance exams can be taken:
ICAR/GATE/AIEEA/ MHT/CET/UPCATET/AP
EAMCET/CG PAT/SHIATS Entrance Exam/CCSHAU
Entrance Exam/Assam Agriculture University
VET/Rajasthan JET/AGRICET
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.

work_environment.places_of_work: Private Offices, Factory, On-Field, Laboratory.
work_environment.work_environment: You will typically work in offices, but Engineer Trainee — Assistant Engineer — Senior may spend time ata variety of worksites, both indoors and Engineer > Operations Manager —> Head of outdoors. You may travel to agricultural settings to see that equipment and machinery are functioning according to the manufacturers’ specifications. Geographical Unit — Head of Company/CEO

careers.example_from_field: Dr. R.K. Aggarwal is a renowned Agricultural Engineer who served with the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) for over three decades. He specialised in dryland agriculture and contributed to the development of improved farming techniques suited to the semi-arid regions of India.

careers.title: Animal Health Worker
careers.description: An Animal Health Worker provides preventive health care, assistance in disease control and basic first aid services to farm animals and poultry. He or she works under indirect or distant supervision of registered veterinarian.

careers.title: Aquaculture Worker
personality_traits: You can determine the procedure of your own work
You like to take care of animals
You would like to start your own business
You are good at planning and work according
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Complete 10+2 with Science stream - Biology as a
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Minimum Class 10 pass or 10+2 in Science stream.
2. Complete a Certificate or Diploma course in Aquaculture, Fisheries Science, or Marine Biology.
OR Complete a B.F.Sc. (Bachelor of Fisheries Science) or B.Sc. in Aquaculture.
You may also enrol in NSQF-level vocational training in Aquaculture.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.career_growth_path: Aquaculture Helper → Aquaculture Worker → Aquaculture Supervisor → Aquaculture Manager → Head of Operations

work_environment.places_of_work: Fish farms, hatcheries, coastal aquaculture facilities, inland water bodies, seafood processing units.
work_environment.work_environment: It is mostly a field-based outdoor job involving work near water. Physical fitness is important. You may work 8-10 hours a day, 6 days a week. Seasonal demands can affect working hours.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: You can start your own aquaculture farm with government support under PMMSY (Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana).

careers.example_from_field: Ratan Mondal from West Bengal started as an aquaculture worker in a local fish farm. Through the PMMSY scheme, he expanded to operate his own brackish water shrimp farm, earning over INR 6 lakh per year and employing five others from his village.

careers.title: Dairy Farmer/Entrepreneur
personality_traits: You like to take care of animals
You would like to start your own business
You are good at planning and work according
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Complete 10+2 with Science stream - Biology as a
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. Enrol in a Diploma or Certificate in Dairy Farming or Animal Husbandry.
OR Complete a B.Sc. in Dairy Science, Animal Husbandry or Veterinary Science.
NALCO, ICAR and state agricultural universities offer relevant programmes.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.career_growth_path: Farm Helper → Dairy Farmer → Dairy Farm Supervisor → Dairy Farm Manager → Dairy Entrepreneur/Owner

work_environment.places_of_work: Dairy farms, cooperative dairy societies, milk processing units, rural areas.
work_environment.work_environment: It is an outdoor, farm-based role requiring early morning work. Physical stamina is needed. Work is 7 days a week as animals require daily care.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: You can start your own dairy farm with support from NABARD and state dairy development boards. Cooperative membership with AMUL or similar cooperatives is also an option.

careers.example_from_field: Shyamlal Pandey, a farmer from Uttar Pradesh, became a successful dairy entrepreneur after joining the Amul cooperative. By adopting improved breeds and scientific feeding practices, he expanded his farm from 5 to 25 cows, tripling his monthly income.

careers.title: Fish and Sea Food Processor
personality_traits: You like to work in teams
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Minimum Class 10 pass or 10+2.
2. Enrol in an NSQF level certificate course in Fish Processing or Food Processing.
OR Complete a Diploma or B.Sc. in Food Technology or Fisheries Science.
Central Institute of Fisheries Education (CIFE) and state fisheries colleges offer relevant courses.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.career_growth_path: Processing Helper → Fish Processor → Supervisor → Quality Inspector → Plant Manager

work_environment.places_of_work: Fish processing plants, seafood export units, canning factories, coastal processing facilities.
work_environment.work_environment: You work indoors in cold and wet processing environments. Physical stamina is required. Shift work is common. Hygiene and food safety protocols must be strictly followed.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: You can start a small-scale seafood processing unit with support from MPEDA (Marine Products Export Development Authority).

careers.title: Dento Oral Hygienist
careers.description: A Dento Oral Hygienist is a dental allied professional who is an expert on oral health, typically focusing on techniques in oral hygiene. Some of the key responsibilities of Dento Oral Hygienists are to remove all sorts of germs from the mouth and to keep the teeth in good condition. They also apply decay preventive agents such as fluorides and sealants, chart medical and dental histories, as well as take and develop dental x-ray films.
